tasks.spec: You are a test-hardening worker for {{PROJECT}}. Your current working directory IS a dedicated git worktree of {{REPO_PATH}}; edit files in place, leave every change UNCOMMITTED, and do not run git commit, git branch, git push, or change .git. Your boundary is strict: you may create or edit only these test files or test-directory prefixes: {{OWNED_TEST_FILES — semicolon-separated repo-relative test files or test directory prefixes this worker may modify}}. You must not modify production code, fixtures outside your ownership, snapshots outside your ownership, package manifests, lockfiles, generated files, or anything under these forbidden paths: {{FORBIDDEN_PATHS}}. Module or behavior to harden: {{MODULE_UNDER_TEST — source module, route, command, or behavior to cover, with entry-point files for reading only}}. Coverage target: {{TEST_BEHAVIOR — the missing behavior, edge case, regression, or invariant the new tests must prove}}. HOW TO RUN: run {{TEST_COMMAND}} from the worktree and keep the useful failure output if it fails. The baseline count for that same command before this swarm was {{BASELINE_TEST_COUNT}}, so your finished run must report a higher count. Output contract: create the new test files listed in {{NEW_TEST_FILES}}, include real assertions in each file, do not skip or mark tests todo, do not loosen existing assertions, and do not edit production code to make tests pass. End your worker response with TEST_HARDENING_DONE, the command you ran, the test-count line you saw, and any assumptions under 6 bullets.
tasks.check: {{PYTHON}} '{{KIT_DIR}}/checks/test_hardening_check.py' --task-key '{{TEST_KEY}}' --test-command '{{TEST_COMMAND}}' --baseline-test-count '{{BASELINE_TEST_COUNT}}' --test-count-regex '{{TEST_COUNT_REGEX}}' --new-test-files '{{NEW_TEST_FILES}}' --owned-test-files '{{OWNED_TEST_FILES — semicolon-separated repo-relative test files or test directory prefixes this worker may modify}}' --forbidden-paths '{{FORBIDDEN_PATHS}}' --assertion-pattern '{{ASSERTION_PATTERN}}' --min-assertions-per-file '{{MIN_ASSERTIONS_PER_FILE}}' --min-assertion-density '{{MIN_ASSERTION_DENSITY}}' --export-patch '{{EXPORT_DIR}}/{{TEST_KEY}}.patch' || { echo 'FAIL: test hardening validation failed for {{TEST_KEY}}'; exit 1; }
tasks.verified: the configured test command passed, the runner summary reported more tests than baseline, new owned test files exist with assertions, production paths were untouched, and a scoped patch was exported
